title: AI Email Assistant
meta_description: XJTLU AI Email Assistant - Automatically search, extract, and summarize email content
hero.default_text: Inbox overflowing? Start with this digest
cookie.title: Connect Campus Mailbox
cookie.hint: Click "Open Login Window" to log in to your campus email in Edge (supports 2FA). Return here and click "I've Logged In". You can also paste Cookie JSON in the advanced section below.
command.title: Generate Email Digest
command.lead: Keywords are optional — leave blank to read the most recent emails. The instruction tells the model what you care about most.
command.gate_hint: Please connect and verify your campus mailbox above first.
command.keyword_placeholder: Leave blank for recent emails; enter keywords to search first
command.instruction_placeholder: What aspects should the digest focus on…
command.instruction_default: Please summarize these emails in a natural tone, focusing on events, coursework, and important matters. Write thorough summaries for each email: include key information, dates/locations, and action items — don't over-compress.
command.email_count_hint: Range 1–10. The model processes in batches then produces a final summary.
deep.summary: Advanced: Deep Scan (up to 100 emails) then filter & analyze
deep.hint: For scanning many emails by category and date, then summarizing at once. Scan the list first (may take several minutes), then filter and click "Analyze Selected Emails".
deep.subhint: After unchecking categories or narrowing dates, the list and "selected" count will update accordingly.
results.title: Digest Results
results.empty: No digests yet. Connect your mailbox and verify, then click "Generate Email Digest" — results will appear below.
security.subtitle: Privacy & Security
security.title: Security Promise
security.item1_pre: Session and credentials are stored in
security.item2_pre: Email body is only used for the current digest,
security.item3_post: you to enter your SSO password here
history.subtitle: Local Records
history.title: Search & Instruction History
digest.subtitle: Automated
digest.title: Scheduled Digest
digest.toggle_hint: After mailbox verification, the system runs digests in the background on the schedule below (may have slight delays when multiple users are active).
digest.time_hint: Digests after this time are based on the most recent emails in the mailbox list, not a strict "past 24 hours" scan.
digest.keyword_placeholder: Leave blank for the latest emails
digest.instruction_placeholder: Tell the AI how to write the digest
digest.note: Scheduled digests always use the "daily" fetch mode, independent of the main area's "Deep Scan".
digest.history_empty: No scheduled digest records yet. Enable and save settings — digests will be generated automatically at the scheduled time.
tutorial.heading: User Guide
tutorial.s1_body: Click "Settings" at the top, fill in the AI Base URL and model name. API Key is optional (leaving it blank will use the system-hosted AI service). Changes take effect immediately.
tutorial.s2_p1: Recommended: In the "Connect Campus Mailbox" section, click "Open Login Window", complete login in Edge, then return here and click "I've Logged In".
tutorial.s2_p2: Advanced: Install Cookie-Editor in Chrome or Edge, log in to your mailbox, export as JSON, and paste it into the "Advanced: Paste Cookie" text box, then save.
tutorial.s2_p3: If the session expires, you can repeat the process above to overwrite and save.
tutorial.s3_li1: When keywords are left blank, the most recent emails are read from the list.
tutorial.s3_li2: After entering a summary instruction, the system will search, extract body text, and generate a digest.
tutorial.s3_li3: Digest results and search history are saved in your browser's local storage (per account).
tutorial.s3_li4: To scan many emails, expand "Advanced: Deep Scan" and follow the two-step guide.
tutorial.s4_p1: Scheduled digests automatically queue a "daily" fetch and summary at your set time — no need to manually click "Generate Email Digest" every day.
tutorial.s4_p2: Click "Scheduled Digest" at the top to open the side panel; "Collapse" in the panel's top-right hides it (saved tasks remain active).
tutorial.s4_li1: Connect mailbox first: Complete the connection and click "Verify Connection" or finish interactive login. If the session expires, scheduled tasks will fail or skip — reconnect and wait for the next cycle.
tutorial.s4_li2: Configure AI: Same as manual digests — fill in an available model endpoint in "Settings" (or use the hosted service).
tutorial.s4_li3: After opening the "Scheduled Digest" panel, enable "Enable scheduled auto-digest", choose "Daily" or "Weekly". For "Weekly", also select which day of the week.
tutorial.s4_li4: Set time: Time is based on the server's local clock (as shown in the page hint). The system queues after that time; actual execution may be delayed by seconds to minutes (background queue, or serialized with ongoing analyses).
tutorial.s4_li5: Keywords (optional): Leave blank to fetch the most recent emails by list order; fill in for keyword search first. "Daily" here means once per cycle, not strictly "every email from the past 24 hours".
tutorial.s4_li6: Summary instruction & count limit: Similar to manual digests — specify focus areas like events, assignments, or notifications; count limit follows daily mode restrictions (shown on page).
tutorial.s4_li7: Click "Save Schedule Settings". After saving, if the feature is enabled, you'll see the "Next approximate trigger" time; you can also click "Refresh" to update the history list.
tutorial.s4_li8: Digest History: The right-side list shows results in reverse chronological order. Click an entry to expand and view the full text; status shows "success", "failed", or "skipped" with explanatory text.
tutorial.s4_note: Note: Scheduled digests always use the "daily" fetch, independent of the main area's "Advanced: Deep Scan". When multiple users are active, tasks run serially and may queue. If a cycle ends due to failure or skip, it usually won't auto-retry within that cycle — wait for the next day/week cycle.
hero_edit.heading: Customize Title Slogan
hero_edit.description: Enter your full slogan below. Optionally specify text to highlight and pick a color — no coding needed. You can also expand "Advanced" to use Markdown/HTML. Content is saved in your browser only.
hero_edit.use_advanced: Use advanced customization (Markdown / HTML) instead of simple formatting above
hero_edit.text_placeholder: e.g., Hey there, time to check your emails!
hero_edit.highlight_placeholder: A segment that appears exactly in the slogan above
hero_edit.advanced_note: HTML will remove script/iframe and other dangerous tags; safe inline styles are allowed. Theme color: var(--accent).
settings.heading: Settings
settings.api_key_placeholder: Leave blank to use system-hosted service
settings.api_key_note: If left blank, the system will use the globally configured DeepSeek endpoint by default.
settings.prelaunch_hint: When enabled, the app will automatically open a browser and log in to the mailbox on startup, reducing wait time for the first query.
settings.dev_summary: Developer Tools (for maintenance — regular users can ignore)
settings.dev_hint: For verifying list and body extraction; uses the main interface's "Search Keywords" and current session. Test 1 writes results to JSON under src/.
settings.dev_test1: Test 1: Extract all bodies (up to 100 emails, no LLM)
settings.dev_test1_hint: Deep list pull (up to 100) scrolls back to list top then incrementally collects; order matches inbox (no date resorting), deduplicates by data-convid; repositions by convid before extraction.
settings.dev_test2: Test 2: Daily workflow body extraction (no LLM)
settings.dev_test2_hint: Same as main interface "daily mode": search_emails(mode=daily) + per-email extract_full_body, up to 10; uses main interface "Search Keywords" and "Email Count" (capped at 10). Results shown below only, no file written.
login.title: Login
login.subtitle: Use the email and password from registration. Email is your unique account identifier.
register.title: Register
register.subtitle: Username is for display; email is the unique account identifier and cannot be duplicated.
js.cookie_format_error: Unrecognized cookie format — please paste Cookie-Editor JSON or Netscape text
js.history_not_found: Corresponding digest record not found — it may have been cleared
js.gate_hint_please_connect: Please connect and verify your campus mailbox above before generating a digest.
js.connecting_mail: Connecting to mailbox — please complete or cancel first
js.cookie_expired_update: Cookie expired — please update in settings
js.deep_hint_mode: Deep mode uses two steps: first "Deep Scan" to pull the list, then filter and "Analyze Selected Emails" (max {n} per analysis).
js.daily_hint_mode: Range 1-10. LLM processes in batches of 3 in parallel, then does 1 final summary.
js.deep_chart_fail: Chart library failed to load — please refresh the page
js.deep_gate_scan: Please connect and verify your campus mailbox above before running a deep scan.
js.deep_gate_analyze: Please connect and verify your campus mailbox above before running analysis.
js.deep_scan_first: Please complete a deep scan first.
js.deep_no_selection: No emails match current filters — adjust categories or dates.
js.deep_too_many: Max {max} emails per analysis — please narrow your filter (currently {n}).
js.deep_scanned_done: Scanned {n} emails — please filter then click "Analyze Selected Emails"
js.deep_no_ai_summary: No AI summary available (API Key not configured or LLM unavailable). You can still use rule-based priorities, cards, and filters below.
js.dev_extracting: Deep-pulling list and extracting all bodies (up to 100 emails), please wait…
js.dev_daily_extracting: Pulling list and extracting bodies in daily mode (no LLM), please wait…
js.dev_daily_done: Daily flow extracted {n} bodies (no LLM called)
js.dev_gate: Please connect and verify your campus mailbox above first.
js.banner_waiting_login: Waiting for you to complete mailbox login in the login window. Return here and click "I've Logged In" when done, or click "Cancel" to abort.
js.gate_connect_first: Please connect your campus mailbox above and complete "Verify Connection" or interactive login before generating a digest.
js.gate_form_locked: The digest form is temporarily unavailable — please complete the mailbox connection step above.
js.digest_load_fail: Failed to load scheduled digest settings
js.digest_default_instruction: Please summarize these emails in a natural tone, focusing on events, coursework, and important matters.
js.summary_deck_aria: Email summary cards — use left/right arrow keys to navigate
js.preset_general_text: Please summarize these emails in a natural tone, focusing on events, coursework, and important matters. Write thorough summaries for each email: include key information, dates/locations, and action items — don't over-compress.
js.preset_detailed_text: Please provide a thorough expanded summary for each email: list key points, preserve important numbers, dates, links, and contacts. Keep a natural tone; err on the side of completeness. If an email is unrelated to campus life/study, still summarize it honestly in its own block.
js.preset_jobs_text: Please focus on recruitment, internships, campus hiring, employment, and career-related emails. List position name, company, deadline, and application method. Briefly mention other types.
js.preset_events_text: Please focus on campus events, lectures, club activities, competitions, and academic conferences. List event name, time, location, and registration method. Briefly note non-event emails.
js.preset_courses_text: Please focus on course notifications, assignment deadlines, exam schedules, grade releases, and add/drop course emails. Sort by urgency, mark each deadline. Briefly mention unrelated emails.
js.preset_deadlines_text: Please extract all items with deadlines, registration cutoffs, payment due dates, and submission deadlines from these emails. Sort chronologically, format: Item Name | Deadline | Source Email Subject. Emails without clear deadlines can be ignored.
js.preset_admin_text: Please focus on emails from school administrative departments (registry, academic affairs, student affairs, finance, etc.). Extract policy changes, procedures, and required actions. Briefly note non-administrative emails.
js.preset_translate_text: Please translate all English emails to Chinese first, then summarize. Summarize Chinese emails directly. Mark the original language for each email. Focus on items requiring reply or action.
js.preset_action_text: Please filter these emails for items that require my personal reply, action, or participation. Ignore pure notifications and promotional emails. For actionable emails, clearly list: what I need to do, by when, and how.
js.history_chip_title: Click: jump to digest result; Shift+Click: fill into form
js.deep_snapshot_saved: Saved to deep_scan_exports/{stem}.json, pie chart saved as .png.
js.deep_snapshot_fail: Snapshot JSON write failed — check server logs.
js.cookie_invalid_toast: Cookie expired or unable to open list
